assertions.expectation: Clearly states that GKE auto-upgrades clusters automatically as the default and recommended model — this is what differentiates GKE from self-managed Kubernetes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE handles upgrades automatically' and 'this is actually the recommended approach for production environments', clearly differentiating from self-managed approaches

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows to control WHEN auto-upgrades happen (e.g., weekends, off-peak hours)
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud commands for setting maintenance windows and recommends configuring them 'during low-traffic periods' with examples showing weekend and off-peak timing

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance exclusions to control WHAT auto-upgrades happen (e.g., 'no minor or node upgrades' to block disruptive changes while allowing security patches)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions at all - no discussion of blocking specific types of upgrades while allowing others

assertions.expectation: Does NOT recommend disabling auto-upgrades or switching to a purely manual upgrade cadence as the first option
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends auto-upgrades as the primary approach and only suggests considering manual upgrades for specific exceptional scenarios

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades are useful for specific scenarios (emergency patches, accelerating the schedule) but are the exception, not the rule
assertions.reason: While it mentions when to consider manual upgrades, it doesn't specifically mention emergency patches or accelerating schedules - focuses on compliance and testing requirements instead

assertions.expectation: Recommends enrolling in scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance) for visibility into upcoming auto-upgrades
assertions.reason: Shows how to enable notification config but doesn't specifically mention 72-hour advance notifications or emphasize their importance for visibility

assertions.expectation: Suggests checking the GKE release schedule for longer-range planning (~1 month best-case for new minors)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention checking GKE release schedules or provide any guidance on longer-range planning timelines

assertions.expectation: Mentions release channel selection as the primary lever for upgrade cadence (Stable = slower, Regular = balanced)
assertions.reason: While it mentions the Regular channel briefly at the end, it doesn't explain release channels as the primary lever for controlling upgrade cadence or compare different channel options
